name: aws-sso-login
description: Authenticate to AWS using Single Sign-On (SSO). Use when AWS CLI operations require SSO authentication or when SSO session has expired.
capabilities: Triggers AWS SSO login command with specified profile
Supports custom profile configuration per project
author: Daisuke897
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation with no executable code. It describes standard AWS SSO authentication procedures using official AWS CLI commands. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting documentation text as code patterns.
valueStatement: AWS SSO sessions expire after 8-12 hours, blocking CLI access. This skill automates the re-authentication process with proper profile handling for seamless cloud operations.
limitations: Requires browser for authentication (cannot be fully automated)
Needs pre-configured AWS CLI v2 and SSO setup
Session duration limited by AWS SSO configuration
Network access to SSO endpoint required

useCases.title: Daily AWS access workflow
useCases.description: Automate morning authentication to AWS accounts via SSO before running infrastructure commands

useCases.title: Session refresh for deployments
useCases.description: Refresh expired SSO sessions during extended deployment or maintenance windows

useCases.title: Switch between AWS accounts
useCases.description: Quickly authenticate to different AWS accounts using various SSO profiles

promptTemplates.title: Basic SSO login
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the aws-sso-login skill to authenticate with my default AWS profile

promptTemplates.title: Specific profile login
promptTemplates.prompt: Authenticate to AWS SSO using the profile 'production-account'

promptTemplates.title: Check current identity
promptTemplates.prompt: After SSO login, run aws sts get-caller-identity to confirm which account I'm authenticated to

promptTemplates.title: Assume role after SSO
promptTemplates.prompt: First authenticate via SSO, then assume the CloudFormation deployment role for account 123456789012

bestPractices: Document default profile names in project README for consistency across team
Check session expiration before long-running operations to avoid mid-process failures
Use specific profiles per environment (dev, staging, prod) for better access control
antiPatterns: Hardcoding profile names in skill invocations - use project configuration instead
Attempting to automate browser authentication - SSO requires manual interaction
Ignoring session expiration warnings - always refresh before critical operations

faq.question: Which AWS CLI versions support SSO?
faq.answer: AWS CLI v2 or later is required for SSO support. Version 1.x does not include SSO functionality.

faq.question: How long do SSO sessions last?
faq.answer: Sessions typically expire after 8-12 hours depending on your organization's AWS SSO configuration.

faq.question: Can this work in headless environments?
faq.answer: Use the --no-browser flag to get a manual URL, but browser authentication is still required.

faq.question: Is my authentication data secure?
faq.answer: Credentials are stored locally in AWS CLI cache and never transmitted to external services.

faq.question: What if authentication fails?
faq.answer: Check your SSO configuration in ~/.aws/config and verify network access to AWS SSO endpoints.

faq.question: How does this compare to aws-azure-login?
faq.answer: This uses native AWS SSO while aws-azure-login is specifically for Azure AD integration with AWS.
